channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 213 replies — 852.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 213 • Updated: 2026-07-20 16:07 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62497795
>>62497528
Sslinkie1 is at it again

62497824
>>62497473
two paths for a crypto
>be full privacy and cyberpunk
>be full authority approved

62498098
>>62497824
2 paths for a link shill
>keep spouting wordsalad buzzword carrot dangles for the next decade
>admit failure and rope

62498297
>>62497824
third option:
>p+c

62498300
>>62498098
wordsalad: "the tech is ready"
realsalad: Chainlink advised SEC on crypto, law makers on Clarity Act
realsalad: Chainlink partner with DTCC, Swift and other institutions
>>62498297
it's going up like usual

62498304
>>62497604
>>62497795
>>62498297
xerpies absolutely seething

62498366
>>62498300
I see you decided on option b

62498376
>>62498366
the buy link option

62498552
>>62498304
>>62498304
you still posting screenshots of your tweets on biz to try and get followers?

62499092
>>62498552
You mean like Adem/Thomas the xerpie has been doing for half a decade?

62499460
>>62496397
Not sure which chart you're looking at...

62501067
>>62466449

https://youtu.be/qAIrj_Vqdfc

62501106
>>62501019
>we know it works. we just don't know what it'll be used for, or how to make it useful in a legal sense, or how to make it work widely enough to be useful.
lol. You guys should start watching David Ike videos. The man can talk for 8 hours nonstop about absolutely nothing, and sound convincing. He's especially gifted at it. These crypto grifters are just doing it to you instead of theaters full of boomers.

62501441
>>62501106
>we just don't know what it'll be used for, or how to make it useful in a legal sense, or how to make it work widely enough to be useful.
She's saying the opposite of all this.

You just don't like what she's saying because it rules out XRP.

62501468
>>62501441
nigger I don't even know what XRP really is. I have no ties to crypto whatsoever. I would never touch anything that specifically appeals to fools who like to be made to feel smart.

62501476
>>62501468
>specifically appeals to fools who like to be made to feel smart
Fools like DTCC and Swift?

62501483
>>62501476
Again, show me any usage. Anything beyond a demo. Literally anything approaching everyday, production usage. Anyone can "partner" with anyone else.

Nigga, people barely even use Bitcoin to do basic transations. In what world does it make sense for some significantly more complex financial product to be based on the extremely heavyweight and super slow foundations of a public ledger? For real. Why not just use the existing financial system which handles a billion transactions a day and has been in constant use and improvement for like 60 years? With regulations and legal support, vast amounts of dedicated infrastructure. Use your head here.

62501538
>>62501483
I understand you're frustrated that XRP isn't involved, but trying to argue on 4chan isn't going to change the minds of DTCC, Swift, ...

62501579
>>62501538
And again, I have no investments or interest in crypto at all. Fuck every single form of crypto. 20 years are going to pass and you are going to end up broke, you retarded bagholding rube. Get out of crypto and just invest like a rational human being.

62501617
>>62501483
I too remain skeptical until I see actual usage, but these partnerships do mean something.

>Why not just use the existing financial system which handles a billion transactions a day and has been in constant use and improvement for like 60 years?
You are looking at one of those improvements anon. Anything that can shave time off of transactions, reduce overhead, reduce complexity or risk will be considered. If it's shit, it will be discarded. Fedwire had been successfully handling transactions telegraphically for 50 years in the 1970s, yet at some point they trialed and switched to Telex and then to computers.

The fact that blockchain tech (in combination with oracles) has survived multiple rounds of trials and demos, with more and more financial institutions setting up teams to build out this stuff, means there is meat to it. How big that change/disruption is remains to be seen, and even then it's not guaranteed you will be able to position yourself in a way that allows you to profit from it. I think Chainlink has a pretty good chance of being that ticket, but we'll see.

62501621
>>62501579
You seem very passionate about Chainlink working with DTCC and Swift.
Some would say even "butthurt".

62501635
>>62501621
I am passionate about pointing out how stupid and empty all of these crypto initiatives are. "Tokenization" is just as retarded as NFTs were, and just as useless. My hope is to break the everyday retard out of wasting their time and futures on anything in the crypto space. People kill themselves because they have lost money and realize it was all a scam. This will be an especially big problem in crypto because part of the reason they are in it in the first place is because they have convinced themselves they are smart because they "understand the tech" when others don't. So when they lose their money and realize that all along they have been legitimately dumb, it's going to be a massacre. I'd like to avoid that by embarassing them out into the real world.

62501638
>>62501635
>gets extremely butthurt at Swift and DTCC
>takes his anger out on 4chan

lel

62501641
>>62501638
Enjoy your lifetime of poverty I guess.

62501644
>>62501641
>DTCC and Swift are stupid and scammy, and it is my personal quest to inform 4chan of this

truly a wholly believable yarn, this

62502089
>my personal quest on 4chan is to shill for DTCC and SWIFT and convince all of you that you should buy my bags, if you dare to push me back or question my shilling narrative or motives you are an XRP bagholder!
the absolute state of retarded shillers

62502131
>>62502089
>my personal quest on 4chan is to shill for DTCC and SWIFT and convince all of you that you should buy my bags, if you dare to push me back or question my shilling narrative or motives you are an XRP bagholder!

62502132
>>62502089
aaaand he switched to his alt ID again

None of this is going to make XRP magically become involved in this.

62502558
>>62502132
I'm not switching ids you mentally ill retard
>70 fucking pbtid

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 151 replies — 604.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 151 • Updated: 2026-07-20 16:09 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62497358
>>62496941
Thank you.

62497362
>>62497334
1 ID = 1 LINK

62497396
>>62497362
Typical ever cuck cope

62497635
Retard they have an enterprise wallet for clients. This post sounds like someone who left after 2024 and is not paying attention to any details

62497666
>>62497635
Good job changing id saar so good

62497753
Hopefully Bob puts me out of my misery one day. I hate managing a daycare full of autistics who think they know everything bc token holder wallet is dog shit. Bob said it last year they don’t have bandwidth for perfect consumer wallet and enterprise wallet so teams time and energy goes into enterprise wallet until they get bigger. So yes it all works, just not for your bitch asses

62497850
>>62497753
Bro just stop coping and put your cuck cage on

62497867
I’m sick of the cuck cage, I want out of this matrix and Everest will do it. All the old bitter holders that took 6 figure losses and clearly have not moved on since they are commenting in here can go fuck themselves

62497871
Idk how to reply directly to someone but to the person shitting on consumer wallet: you would not be here commenting on Everest 4chan post if you had any bit of success in crypto. Whatever you find next will also rugpull on you until you sell then take off just like ID well

62497958
>>62497867
You do understand that everyone sees your 4ch ID, right? Right???

62497995
Yes you stupid goy I am nWDGiV1T. I’m sorry but you guys are pathetic fudders, try harder

62498030
>>62497958
>47 posts by that ID
KEK how heavy you think that fucks bags are

62498225
>>62498030
The heaviest bag. From his writing style, I know this anon is this one 'animal' user from everest telegram who bought since ICO, held through ATH, bought the token every month despite the chart & project silence, and diligently stake everytime even after knowing that stake rewards never ever outpace the price decline, and never sell.

He is not a whale, but his bag definitely cost the most. It was funny to see his unwavering belief and occasional tantrum, but now I can only feel pity.

62498287
>>62461988
they will all get MICA in the end because they need to tax everything

62498311
Is that why Binance could not get it and a shit ton of other coins that preached compliance. Sure thing brother, everyone gonna get it in the end for taxed. Dumb ass MiCA 2.0 is even stricter, it’s not getting easier from here idiot hahahahhaa. Most of Defi will be wiped out or forced to use licensed payment solutions

62498556
Sipping on some woodford reserve because I know I will have the last laugh here. Nobody laughs at me and calls it a day, I will be right in the end. Can’t wait to revisit this thread in 2030, have a nice night all

62498568
>>62498556
You will be bald by then retard

62498575
I actually do not stress and try to meditate/energy harvest for ID token so my full set of hair will still be around once bull flag is complete end of 2030

62498583
I am the whale, and I am a very nice whale with diamond hands. Any other new whales are more than welcome here, 1.4m mc with MiCA CASP to operate in Europe. They will be attacking DTCC settlements with that Kick Start Award from Stellar as the in. 99.5% down from ATH, I’m literally doing you idiots a favor and NOBODY is doing an inch of research and just calling me a bag holding retard instead. Do you know how fucking frustrating that feels to me?? Search Everest ID on Twitter and look at Teams posts from last 5 years, that is good start. This project is over most people’s heads, it takes too much time to fully understand it so the retards chase memes instead. One day, the Yin Yang will balance out in crypto too

62499388
>>62498583
its time to sell

62499407
My brother in Christ, my target is many dollars, not 1 cent

62501683
>>62499407
realistic prediction?

62501718
>>62501683
Do you know how easy it is to scale what they have now? Dollars is easily within reach

62501779
In 2024 it ran to 40 cents and I think they are fundamentally stronger now with CASP; adding multiple EMI, banking, and card-processing partners in 2025. 2021 ATH is 2.28, I think it retests that at some point next few years, idk the ONLY thing is team does not advertise or market shit, that is only blocker imo. I think it’s a great DYOR. Very simple search Everest ID on X and go through team’s posts/newsletters. Founder Bob is also a relentless mfer who would love to give fat middle finger to all the haters last 5 years, to each their own

62502264
>>62501779
>the ONLY thing is team does not advertise or market shit

62502480
Kek I think that was from pilot partnership they had with Taco Bell. They also dipped their foot in DEI with Liza. Can’t wait to see what they come up with next

62502482
Try to apply to a job at Oracle Inc...exact same email sign in...wtf

62502537
>>62502482
Link?

62502561
I think he means this: https://signon.oracle.com/signin

It looks similar to Everest sign in but not enough for me to make any conclusions. I dug to death on this multiple times and found NOTHING regarding Everest and Oracle post 2021 so I am almost positive it is dead. Bob never explicitly confirmed one way or other but there would have to be even 1 or 2 cookie crumbs if someone deep dove as much as possible, I found nothing post 2021.

62502568
The only thing I found weird (likely pure coincidence) is Everchain randomly went private for “security reasons” then a few weeks later Oracle went live with the Financial Crime and Compliance Management Service that Everest said they partnered with them to build. Bobs Oracle contact left the next year so most likely nothing.

item.title: As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy… — 79 replies — 316.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy…

Open thread • Replies: 79 • Updated: 2026-07-20 16:04 UTC

OP

As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy from us. You get a Yuan swap line. You settle trade imbalances in gold or Yuan and use our settlement systems.

What can the US do? All they have is threats against the likes of Argentina. China has the vital goods they need at the right prices. The will continue to do this and kill dollar dominance.

Theres no other choice or enter an inflationary spiral.

Early replies (first 3)

62488083
how does this play out in 20 years? half the countries buy using Yuan? US goes back to manufacturing cuz dollar falls? Americans finally lose weight?

62488086
>>62488026
I thought Milei was anti-Chinese?

62488137
>Argentine peso devalues to zimbabwe-tier levels
>Hard for businesses to even buy USD because the market is so shallow and the currency so unwanted price discovery becomes tricky
>Milei's lolbertarian stance does little to appease markets, forex remains tightly regulated to avoid massive capital flights
>country with almost no industry or advanced services to export
>USD reserves run dry
>Great opportunity for China to dump it's surplus production, will probably be selling shit to Argentina at a loss in a government-set artificial exchange rate just as they do with Veneuela and African shitholes that struggle to access USD
>brown thirdie posts his paid shilling painting this as a massive victory for china and a sign of the dollar collapsing
We should have flags on /biz/. I'd call you a chink but I don't think chinks can even access this site.
>As I was saying yesterday.
Your thread died within 24hs on one of the slowest boards on this site. That should tell you something. Fuck off paid brown tankie.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62497713
>>62488137
>a triple digit IQ post on /biz/
R A R E
A
R
E

62497725
I still don't understand how being a "global reserve currency" brings any benefit to me or the country

thanks

62497790
>>62497725
you'll find out sooner than later, you ignorant faggot

62497813
>>62488083
The bottom 90% of American are very poor and top 10% very rich.
Bottom 90% very brown and black and mixed.
Similar to what we have now but worse.

62497953
>>62488083
I hope so, even if we're 100% insular America could have one of the highest standards of living.

62498057
>>62493633
>The solution is the asset controlled by chinese data centers.
brilliant!

62498075
>>62488086
He is just a nihilist jew who wants to see Batman dead.

62498206
>>62488137
EXTREMELY good post.

62498282
>>62496252
They publicly SAID they are going for it. Meanwhile, they are stacking gold in unknown but very large amounts, streamlining gold import/export regs, opening exchanges, and curtailing paper gold trading. Does that all seem like random events to you? Doesn't to me.

62498284
>>62497162
How much value has the dollar lost in those twenty years?

62498292
>>62497725
It soaks up currency so the government/banks can create and spend massive quantities of new currency with minimal inflation.

62498381
>>62498282
The Chinese operate on an extremely long time scale. “Going for it” could be “In fifty years we aim to be the world reserve currency.” I have no doubt they’re going for it; they’re going for everything. The fundamental problem they face is that they are still an export economy and are not yet equipped to be the global financial hub (hell, there is barely an alternative to SWIFT and even then, its only used in a few trades between countries). They still manage their currency and artificially inflate/deflate the yuan as they see fit. I understand the argument that they want to be gold-backed, but that’s a long ways away. You’re jumping the gun.

62498391
>>62498292
yea so that non-whites can get welfare and boomers can get health care

62498444
>>62498391
The non-meme answer to your question is that much of America’s modern history is inseparable from its identity as the world reserve currency. The property, infrastructure, quality of life, etc. that you may feel are decaying are decaying *relative* to how good things were, and “how good things were” is the result of being the world reserve currency. So you may look around now and say you don’t see the benefits, but the reality is that all of contemporary American life is in the shadow of a litany of benefits, decades and decades of benefits.

62498492
>>62498381
To evaluate China's strategy, you must take into account how badly the USA is managing its financial system. Really all China needs to do is sit back and wait while the US burns down its own house. All roads lead to massive inflation of the USD. At some point the world is going to start feeling the pain and then China, massive exporter that it is, will only need to say "10% discount for paying in our new gold-backed yuan." Then 20%. Then 50%. Then "We no longer accept USD." It might take a few years, but the USA is incapable of stopping it. No shooting, no shouting, just economic power, and the fruit of decades of American greed and corruption.

It WILL happen. You say "long ways away". Maybe. I say the interest on the US national debt will consume ALL Federal revenue in less than ten years. And that will be the turning point (if we make it that far). The bridge is out and there is zero political will to stop the train. And when the USD crashes, China will be ready to pick up the pieces.

62498507
>>62498391
You are seeing the end days. From WWII until the early 80s, America had an economic boom like the world had never seen and it was a flood that raised all ships. But it was unsustainable. It gave us the k-shaped economy. And now it is breaking.

China saw that prosperity and wants it. Keep in mind that the CCP has made some colossal blunders with its central planning. And it will likely eventually bungle a gold-backed currency. But before that happens, gold stackers will do very nicely thank you.

62498511
>>62498444
This is correct.

62498595
>>62497725
Try being british or Swedish and you'll see what it's like to have a weak currency

62498877
>>62488026
>>62496108
>China wants that. They have said it publicly.
then why is the CNY/USD rate fixed? 1 USD = 7 CNY

>>62498282
>They publicly SAID they are going for it.
post source. also, post your bags of CNY.

this whole argument falls when you consider that single fact.

62499351
>>62498492
Your timeline is just too aggressive. Debt on interest will not surpass GDP in ten years.
>Then "We no longer accept USD." It might take a few years, but the USA is incapable of stopping it. No shooting, no shouting
This is where you’re definitely wrong. There will be shooting. If you think the US will politely surrender its financial position and quietly slip into economic collapse, you are mistaken.

62499580
>>62488026
Milei is Israeli

62499628
>>62498877
Took two seconds: https://www.channelnewsasia.com/east-asia/xi-jinping-yuan-currency-us-dollar-china-5920046

My bags are in picrel.

62499648
>>62499351
Do the math. Assumptions: current trends in Federal spending continue. Current trends in Federal revenue continue. Current trends in interest rates continue. One third of very low interest US treasury paper rolls over this year into much higher rates. You don't even have to speculate that current holders of treasuries will shy away as the end approaches or that Federal revenue is going to drop as the recession continues. MMT is in its death spiral.

Who are you going to shoot? Going to nuke China? The US military can't even intimidate Iran. Killing people can only delay but never defy the laws of economics.

62499671
>>62499648
How about you do some math here. Do you see the number on the left side of this graph? You’re saying that number will be 100+ in ten years. Does that look right to you?

62499676
>>62499648
And yes, to answer your second question. The US will flip out and bomb the entire planet before they relinquish their role and settle into total economic collapse. If you really believe that’s not a threat at all, more power to you.

62499873
>>62499628
>Qiushi, an official publication of the Communist Party, recently shared excerpts from a hitherto-unpublished 2024 speech by Xi to officials.
>2024 speech by Xi
so, years ago. it still hasn't happened.

>My bags are in picrel.
I only see gold and guns there. 0 yuans.
so you don't put your money where your mouth is.

62499920
I don't know why 4chan hasnt ip range banned anyone located in China yet but until they do we're going to get more threads full of lies like this one.

OP I hope you can feed your family with that yuan you get for posting this. To everyone else in the world its completely valueless.

62499930
>>62495485
1. Argentina has the sixth largest Jewish community
2. Argentina made plans to accommodate 300k Israeli refugees of war
3. Mekorot, a company that supplies 90% of the drinkable water in Israel, is present in Argentina
4. Wildfires in Patagonia at the end of 2025 and beginning of 2026, thought to be started by Israeli tourists, at the same time Milei modifies a law that permits foreigners to buy land
5. Milei's first visit abroad as president was Chabad Lubavitch World Headquarters in Brooklyn, the same place as the tunnel incident

62502541
>>62495499
Checked
They are being saved in one regard, only to have their entire future in peril of being sold out
They are in the midst of rearranging the state as per the usual faustian covenant

62502546
>>62498057
China banned mining.

item.title: Strait out of Mandab HWA (Houthis with attitude) edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners:… — 61 replies — 244.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Strait out of Mandab HWA (Houthis with attitude) edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners:…

Open thread • Replies: 61 • Updated: 2026-07-20 16:04 UTC

OP

Strait out of Mandab HWA (Houthis with attitude) edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars:
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Best Investing:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/VTI/

>Misc:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>previous
>>62501114

Early replies (first 3)

62501883
>he forgot to but /smg/ the title
NIGGER

62501885
>>62501883
*put

62501887
> futures

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62501982
>>62501942
There are many commentators and hosts, both for 4chan cup and /vg/ league. And invitationals. But it seems that /biz/ team doesn't have a manager anymore.

62501986
>>62501982
No shit we don't have a manager. Ever since the "sa'ar" email debacle this board is just 99% scamming indians pushing random shitcoins

62501989
Americans love their football because they think it's like war, but then they still lose every one. muh tactics

62501992
>>62501970
Why are IBM software earnings spoiler'd?
What will be in the report?

62501998
should i get out before americans dump?

62502005
two straits are now closed and WTI is at $81, maybe the schizos going on about bessent shorting weren't wrong because how the fuck is that possible

62502009
no bang bang bang? ;;;;

62502010
>>62502003
based

62502013
One green open after a steep downswing and bears are already whining again lmao

62502015
oh shit I didn't realise OP is a faggot who didn't bake with a topic

62502016
no more straits today I am....le gay....

62502017
>>62502005
>shizos
>when he said it was what he's going to do
>when you know he's doing it and have still lost so much money and still putting in more knowing its only a matter of time until he wins

i don't understand oilers

62502020
>>62502013
Nothing is enough for those sluts.

62502023
>>62502017
>when you know he's doing it and have still lost so much money
i bought oil equites late 2025 the war premium being shaved on or off doesn't mean anything to me lol

62502025
Let's just pump for no reason. I'm long and short, optionsfarming but this still doesn't really make sense

62502028
>>62502009
check the other thread

62502044
is getting real time options data worth it? the 10-20 minute delay fills me with fomo but the fat IV stays fat all day so it feels like it's only useful on open

62502046
Closed out that big soxl trade for 5k profit babes. Feeling good about it.

62502050
>>62502025
numba went down therefore it go up again

62502055
>>62502044
my broker gives deep real time options data by default. let me guess. robinhoodcuck?

62502059
>>62502046
Or i guess closer to 6.5k, struggling with accounting. Gonna put some on ice in sgov for taxes but i have a good amount of cash rn

62502069
>>62502055
nope
ibkriminal

62502079
>>62502044
no just use limit orders and you're on the same playing field as everyone else

62502081
Debating whether it's worth getting back in to Kioxia now. Down 50% from last months ATH, but I think Japs will still dump it lower.

62502096
>>62502081
Buy it tomorrow morning after the Asian sellside gets exhausted.

62502115
Gemini gave me a lot of advice on trading, told me that sminiconfluxers likely keep going down as long as there is mideast fighting, but that everyone already knows that and there is aggressive short selling going on, bit sometimes the big boys paint the tape higher in prenarket to try to squeeze them out and get higher bids for their next dump, which i think is what happenes today. Keeps saying to wait for a high volume capitulation flush which could come mod august -_-'
Cant harm me for reals because i am now off the hook, but i own a ton of intc as you all know and i dont like watching number go down for weeks. It might be wrong, you never know, but it gave me good advice on exiting this bad and dangerous trade for a good profit

62502125
>>62502115
gemini deals in the real world smartass

62502141
>>62502115
>Gemini gave me a lot of advice on trading,

62502183
>>62502017
My oil was bought literally in March 2020. Doesn't change that what we're witnessing is turboretarded, and no matter how many cocks Bussy sits on and no matter how much tax money he burns to manipulate shit, it won't print actual oil molecules. Heck, we ARE witnessing exactly that with the refined products margis going through the roof. Actual products.

62502549
>>62502059
Must suck having to pay taxes and keep track of that. Glad all my funds are in iras

item.title: Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in no… — 81 replies — 192.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in no…

Open thread • Replies: 81 • Updated: 2026-07-20 15:47 UTC

OP

Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in now.

Early replies (first 3)

62483359
>>62483350
4%

62483366
>>62483359
?

62483376
>>62483366
!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62493017
>how did you arrive at that price target

62493049
>>62492529
That's why it went public. To sell the AI top, and get CAPEX moneis. He needed to piggyback xAI on two hyper successful, wide moat businesses.

62493824
>>62490969
It's probably the other way around. Terrestrial networks will cover the bulk of the market, and satellite will likely remain a niche market for wherever terrestrial coverage is poor.

Laser interconnections in the vacuum of space are lower latency due to the higher speed of light in vacuum, although hollow core fiber mostly negates that advantage

62493846
>>62493049
What's the wide moat of Starlink? Competitors are already launching. The competitors will have fewer satellites, which means they will be able to serve fewer customers per square kilometer, but that doesn't really matter much, because the bulk of the space internet market is low population density regions.

62494909
>>62492909
>>62492905
>>62492490
I'm telling you. BUY SPCX OR YOU'LL REGRET IT!

62495185
>>62483350
>$135 is the absolute floor
any updates?

62495365
>>62495185
$120 is the floor for real

62496083
>>62494909
>>62495365
So this is what too much cocaine and special K does to the brain huh?

62497248
>>62483350
>bear case
>+57%

62497262
I would make you fat fags buy my bags if I could.

62497303
These spaceX threads always destroy my sides of laughter when this shit hit $50 a stock imma implode.

62497308
>>62487453

>rural areas

They’ve been saying this stupid shit since 2001. Are you a fucken zoomer or something? If the rural areas do need it, how the fuck can they afford starlink which is $300/mo. You stupid fucken double nigger

62497323
floor bros....we're approaching the basement

62498562
>only 5% float

62499311
oof this didn't age well

62499315
Quite literally almost every SPCX holder is a baggie right now

62499329
I've got 50p, fuck you, bastard

62500590
>>62499311
>>62499315
>>62499329
it will be $200 by end of the year. guaranteed

62501192
>>62500590
Ok. Why?

62501206
Maybe you're right, but I've seen too many "guaranteed bottoms" fail before. Risk management still comes first. Candora's Order Shield will briefly evaluate sudden price spikes before triggering stop orders once it launches.

62501574
we are so back

62501731
>>62483884
this. website owner obviously going to shill his heaviest bags

62502348
Floor is in. Once in a lifetime opportunity right now.

62502364
>>62502348
indeed.

62502366
>>62483350
>Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor.
Fact check: False

62502421
what do you call it when a carcrash meets a rocket explosion? The absolute Unholy Mess of these mountains of Muskshit meeting would be incredible to behold. Biggest Corp. Bankruptcy, Eva. Easily.

62502469
>>62486626

62502473
>>62502364
>the upside is fathomably inconceivable

item.title: Most notable reddit investment factions — 29 replies — 112.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Most notable reddit investment factions

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-07-20 15:57 UTC

OP

I have lurked in reddit for like 3 days and i found 3 notable investment factions that hate each other.

1. WSB - you already know this homos.
2. Bogleheads - Boomers and millenials, won't call them retarded.
3. Dividend gangs
Interesting people. They hate 1 and 2.
They think buying voo is gambling and is similar to bitcoins because they rely on future price changing.
They want to own stale on free cahsflow and get bennies,
and reinvest bennies to get more bennies.
They say if you need to sell your stonks after you retire you are wrong.

How do you guys think about 3?
They seem kind of heterodox.

Early replies (first 3)

62496098
>>62496092
>2. Bogleheads - Boomers and millenials, won't call them retarded.
They're retarded also when we say millennials who the fuck are we talking about, because this board used to be 90% millennials gambling on crypto in 2017. Do we mean "millennial" (born 1981) or millennial (born 1993)?

62496113
>>62496098
Their bogle people seem to have more older millenials, but not sure.
I think they also have some gen zs as some people keep asking what to do with their small wages.

62496115
>>62496113
>le career progression meme
I'm out.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496714
cash crew #1
everyone hates us
CASH CREW REPRESENT

62496720
>>62496714
Fuck yeah bro lets hijack this thead for the cash crew

62496816
i go to plebbit normie financial advice reddits and be the only only one telling these ETF idiots to buy gold and silver (a good safe too which in itself is a good investement). They sperg, mald and cope everytime.

62497237
>>62496092
3 sounds absolutely retarded low T and risk adverse. If you think VOO is too risky you need to blast tren to save your nutless bag between your legs. You also have to consider what the alternative to NOT investing is, which is sitting in fiat, which is destined to go down no matter what from our money printing usurious economic system. So not only are they low T, risk adverse losers, they are also retarded.

62497244
>>62496816
>saaar pls buy my bags at cyclical decade highs saaaaaaaaaar
Indian housewife detected.

62497249
>>62496092
>They think buying voo is gambling and is similar to bitcoins because they rely on future price changing.

This is dumb. VOO goes up because it creates actual wealth. Not so for BTC.

62497259
>>62497249
VOO goes up because the stocks go up, which has little correlation with their revenue and even less with their actual valuation as companies. They go up because dollar go down. Just like Gold and Silver too.

62497271
>>62496092
>dividends reinvested are retarded

62497273
>>62496092
3 are essentially communists of the market.

1 and 2 are trying to find the underlying pattern of human behavior/investment so they at least have my respect for that.

62497338
>>62497271
Why? If you don't need the money and you want maximum growth, it makes sense.

62497357
>>62496714
Ayo!

62497367
>>62496092
I still dont know what a boglehead is. Is it just DCA into index funds? Typically these people win out long term, statistically.

62497369
>>62496092
>Dividend gangs
These people are 2 digit IQ retards that are math challenged. Basically, if stock A is $100 and will go to $110 in a year and appreciate 10% thereafter, but stock B is $100 and pays $2 (2%) in dividends a year and appreciates 5% annually, dividend retards will take stock B.

To them the idea of having shares and getting more shares and not selling the shares and the shares just give them dividends that is free money is somehow better than shares of stock that is ultimately worth more and they just sell a few shares of, because eventually one will run out of shares. Pretty much boy math if there ever was one.

62497372
>>62496816
Sir, every investment forum has been malding at me for 5 years recommending metals. They think their decades high 2-3% yield is somehow a much safer play. They have no idea $5k is still very cheap. Their point of reference over the last 50 years is about to leave them in tears

62497373
>>62496092
It's because if you do #2 the second you stop working / accumulating you have to start selling and it will eventually eat away at everything, so you have to be very strict only 4% max and even that can rekt you.
#3 seems like you can get the best of both worlds, you preserve ownership and live off the cash flow, but it's all the same shit in the end.
Or is it, preserving ownership is pretty powerful as opposed to the mathematical certainty of eventually losing ownership.
I wish reit income were taxes like ordinary dividends.

62497376
>>62497372
If youre young you should not be buying gold, or betting on silver. Save that for when youre 70

62497387
>>62497376
I am glad I didn’t listen to this advice 5 years ago. I’m glad I won’t be listening to this advice in the future. Metals are undergoing a once in a generation remonitization and it’s the comfiest and easiest hold than any paper

62497395
>>62497387
Dude, I own silver. I just dont put everything into it because you genuinely shouldnt do that.

62497397
>>62497338
QED.

62497416
>>62496092
bogling can be good for growth investment, but the goal should always be to have as much non-sale income as possible. if you're not going to have enough income then start selling shares but otherwise keep them.
starting with divvies has its own advantages especially if you get laid off.

62497433
>>62497369

>>62497373
Retard in point

62497502
>>62496098
How is 81 a "millennial"? That's where millennial started and its been that way from the beginning

62497612
>>62497433
Well refute the point faggot. You can't

62498469
>>62497612
>you have to start selling and it will eventually eat away at everything

Yeah there's the short bus, the feeling that a dividend is eternal and therefore better to have beyond the sheer expected value.

62501118
I used to be dividend gang but that was retarded. Have gone full boglehead have 20 years left until retirement but I am aimed to be at 3.2 million all in. ROTH when I hit 60.

Just going to live off the 2% (64k a year) and let it grow from 60 to 70. At 70 my bond ladder hits along with my upping from 2% to 4% along with social security turns my income up to anywhere from 160k to 210k a year depending on the market.

62502526
>>62501118
What do you need 200k a year for at 70 years old? have you ever actually met a 70 year old before or are you just making things up for a laugh?

its down to 60k like it was years ago what a shitty outcome for you hahaha no growth lmao you suck at financial prowess

>>62502261
I call your mother sister a group. I fucking your dad in ass hole. Why you run like lady, eh?

62500695

62500792
>>62500393

62500831
What is it 1992? If you want to all in go for SPCX.

62500834
>>62500393
LEROOOOOOOOOOY JENKINS

62500835
>>62500481
>dude you have to buy coca cola, walmart, home depot, general electric, some chinese company and other shitters because diversity
If I never listened to retards like you I woulda put 100k into leveraged WDX in january and would have 8xed my folio

>>62500695
if you sold this 'double top' you'd have missed the next leg up

62500847
>>62500509
I had a mix of S&P500, CNR, and some A.I ETFs, I sold because I thought it was a bubble but Microsoft was trading at a P/E of 20 when I bought.

a 5% yield for a company growing profits at 20% and expected by analysts to continue into 2030 is a damn good deal imo.

>>62500579
Lmao if you did any research you would be buying Microsoft right now.

62500855
But unironically, where do you think is the price going short-term? 440?

62500867
>>62500855
I'm thinking $450 within a year from now.

62500868
>>62500393

62500870
>>62500868

62500875
>>62500870

62500877
>>62500867

It's like 3 months max you know.

62500887
>>62500877
I have no idea, desu I look at 3 years minimum.

62501060
>>62500393
oh wow let me guess, the asha gotcha bullshit got you to buy. fucking retard. spencer did way too much dmg to the xbox gaming scene. it aint coming back.

62501095
>>62500393
based

62502286
>>62500393
Most of my money is in microsoft too. I started high and kept averaging down these last months. Last thursday I finally managed to sit in the green for the first time in a long time and it's a very awkward situation. Rationally it's a company with strong fundamentals and a very robust and rooted base, but every fiber in my body screams me to fuck off as soon as possible now that I still can. I want to wait until they sit at 450 but that could take fucking years at this point and I'd rather sell everything before the next recession truly hits the stock market and I lose months or even years of progress.

62502304
>>62500393
do not redeeem all your money in 1 gift card saar

62502316
People are actually bullish on jeetsoft? Their current OS is so shit that NORMIES are switching to Linux.

62502403
>>62502316
I'm confident on them managing to do a big squeeze one last time, but not much beyond that. I'm in until that happens or if I get into -5% profits or lower again.
